stories.headline: Lebanon 'separate skirmish' and not part of Iran ceasefire deal, Trump tells US media
stories.summary: Israel has rarely struck central Beirut since the outbreak of the latest Israel-Hezbollah war on 2 March but has regularly struck southern and eastern Lebanon and Beirut's southern suburbs.

stories.headline: Iran ceasefire deal gives Trump a way out of war - but at a high cost
stories.summary: The path to the two-week ceasefire with Iran may have fundamentally altered the way the rest of the world views the US.

stories.headline: At least 182 killed across Lebanon in large wave of Israeli strikes
stories.summary: Attacks hit the southern suburbs of Beirut, southern Lebanon and the eastern Bekaa Valley, hours after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced.

stories.headline: Oil prices plunge and shares jump on US-Iran ceasefire plan
stories.summary: Crude prices tumbled by as much as 15% on the conditional pause but remain far higher than before the war.

stories.headline: Ceasefire offers Trump exit as Iran war becomes political liability
stories.summary: The ceasefire deal with Iran might come at the right moment for Donald Trump, as it could stop the erosion of domestic support for the President. But Trump is left facing scrutiny over why and for what did the US get into war.

stories.headline: How Pakistan helped secure a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran
stories.summary: Pakistan has a historic relationship with Iran, but an agreement was far from certain, writes the BBC’s Caroline Davies

stories.headline: Is conditional ceasefire a victory for US or Iran?
stories.summary: BBC correspondent Frank Gardner explains the current situation as both sides claim victory - Iran says it will reopen the Strait of Hormuz and that its "victory in the field would also be consolidated" in upcoming talks.

stories.headline: White House says Trump’s ‘red line’ against Iran nuclear enrichment remains
stories.summary: Trump's spokesperson says Vice President JD Vance will lead US team for negotiations with Iran in Pakistan on Saturday.

stories.headline: Middle East ceasefire under pressure over Israeli strikes on Lebanon
stories.summary: Bombardment prompted Iran to halt passage of oil tankers through Strait of Hormuz

stories.headline: What we know about the two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran
stories.summary: The provisional truce comes more than a month after the US and Israel launched coordinated attacks on Iran.

stories.headline: Iran Strait of Hormuz warning adds to shipping uncertainty
stories.summary: Only a few vessels have crossed the strait since the US-Iran ceasefire deal, according to BBC Verify analysis.

stories.headline: Why ceasefire deal with US has unsettled Iran's hardliners
stories.summary: The two-week truce opens up the prospect of direct talks with the US but has angered Iran's hardliners, writes BBC News Persian’s Kasra Naji.

stories.headline: Has Trump Met His War Goals in Iran?
stories.summary: On the first day of the pause, Iran fired missiles and launched drones in the region. It said an oil refinery on Lavan Island had been attacked. Israel continued its strikes in Lebanon.

stories.headline: Trump says Lebanon not included in US-Iran ceasefire amid Israeli assault
stories.summary: US president says war in Lebanon is 'separate skirmish' as Israel kills dozens across the country after regional truce.

stories.headline: JD Vance slams Zelenskyy comments on Orban ahead of Hungary election
stories.summary: US vice president in Hungary calls Ukrainian leader's 'threatening' remarks 'completely scandalous'.

stories.headline: Trump-backed Republican Clay Fuller wins election to replace Marjorie Taylor Greene
stories.summary: He will serve out the rest of Marjorie Taylor Greene's term, which ends in January 2027.

stories.headline: Top Australian soldier charged with war crimes to remain in jail on remand
stories.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith faces five charges of the war crime of murder, which has a penalty of life in prison.

stories.headline: Clay Fuller, Trump Ally, Will Take Marjorie Taylor Greene’s Seat in Georgia
stories.summary: Democrats had hoped for a strong showing, but the conservative district in Northwest Georgia elected Clay Fuller after President Trump endorsed him.

stories.headline: Polls in Wisconsin Reflect a More Muted Supreme Court Race
stories.summary: This year’s election has featured more undecided voters and lower overall awareness compared with recent cycles.

stories.headline: Republican wins Marjorie Taylor Greene seat but by tighter margin
stories.summary: Clay Fuller, backed by President Donald Trump, defeated Democrat Shawn Harris by a much smaller margin than what Republicans enjoyed in previous years in the deep-red district vacated by Marjorie Taylor Greene.

stories.headline: He's Australia's most decorated soldier. Now he's at the centre of a historic war crimes case
stories.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith's case is not only unprecedented for Australia but "extraordinary" for the globe too, historians say.

stories.headline: Bondi Won’t Appear on Capitol Hill for Scheduled Epstein Deposition

stories.headline: Voters expand liberal majority on nationally watched Wisconsin Supreme Court
stories.summary: Wisconsin is home to close elections and contentious ideological battles, and its high court could take up cases on abortion, redistricting and election disputes in coming years.

stories.summary: Leaked calls show Hungary's Foreign Minister briefing Russia's Lavrov during a key EU summit, offering confidential EU documents to Moscow, and arranging a meeting between Orbán and Vladimir Putin.

stories.headline: Railway track broke day before train accident that killed 46, Spain's Civil Guard says
stories.summary: The accident happened when a Madrid-bound service run by private firm Iryo derailed and crossed onto the opposite track, smashing into an oncoming train operated by state company Renfe.

stories.headline: Gambia appoints British barrister to prosecute gruesome Jammeh-era crimes
stories.summary: Ex-President Yahya Jammeh's 22-year rule was known for enforced disappearances and extrajudicial killings.

stories.headline: Coldwell's Waugh: Uncertainty Driving Housing Market

stories.headline: Chile Uncovers Criminal Networks Shipping Millions of Dollars of Copper to Peru and China
stories.summary: Chilean investigators have exposed how organized criminal groups steal, process and ship copper on a previously unreported scale as perpetrators seek to capitalize on high metal prices.

stories.headline: Stocks Rally, Oil Falls on Iran Truce | Closing Bell
stories.summary: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Canadian Oil Fetches Biggest Gulf Coast Premium in Two Years
stories.summary: It is the most lucrative time in more than two years to ship Canadian crude to the US Gulf Coast, thanks to the effective closing of the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Tradeweb CEO: Opportunities Around Predictive Markets
stories.summary: Tradeweb CEO Billy Hult discusses the bond trading market and the room for opportunities in predictive markets. He talks with Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Higher Oil Costs to Mute Some Dealmaking: Zimmer
stories.summary: Matt Zimmer, global head of investment banking at William Blair, joins Dani Burger and Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Deals." They discuss which sectors stand to see a slowdown or acceleration in dealmaking amid global fuel constraints.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Democrat Wins Mayoral Race in Republican-Leaning Waukesha, Wis.
stories.summary: Alicia Halvensleben, the president of the city’s Common Council, defeated a Republican legislator, continuing a string of Trump-era mayoral wins for Democrats.

stories.summary: The vice president traveled to Budapest as Trump’s deadline for an Iran deal loomed Tuesday, backing the administration’s closest ideological ally in Europe.

stories.headline: Why Real Madrid tie could make or break Kane's Ballon d'Or hopes
stories.summary: Harry Kane is the leading goalscorer in Europe but does he need to win a major trophy to have a chance of winning the prestigious award?

stories.headline: After clashes with Hegseth, Army secretary says he isn’t going anywhere
stories.summary: Dan Driscoll, praised by the White House, said in a statement to The Washington Post that “I have no plans to depart or resign as the Secretary of the Army.”

stories.headline: Watch: Who is the coup leader who will be Myanmar's next president?
stories.summary: Min Aung Hlaing presided over his last military parade in Myanmar before he takes up the role of president.

stories.headline: Trump leaves key questions unanswered as he seeks to calm nerves over Iran war
stories.summary: There were some glaring omissions in the US president's primetime address, writes the BBC's Gary O'Donoghue.

stories.headline: Fears cost of water and beer to soar as India's scorching summer hits
stories.summary: Makers of plastic and glass bottles are struggling to access raw materials as the war squeezes supplies.

stories.headline: Macron calls Trump's remarks on his marriage 'inelegant'
stories.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron has said Donald Trump's comments about his marriage were "neither elegant nor up to standard".
